contentBlocks.title: Why convert JSON to CSV?
contentBlocks.html:
JSON is the standard data exchange format for REST APIs, web applications, and NoSQL databases. However, for data analysis, reporting, and SQL database imports, you need a tabular format. CSV opens directly in Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc, and every other spreadsheet application — no additional software needed.

Converting JSON to CSV transforms an array of JSON objects into a flat table where object keys become column headers and values become rows. This is essential when you download data from APIs (Stripe, Shopify, Google Analytics) and need to analyze it in a spreadsheet or import it into a relational database (MySQL, PostgreSQL).

The converter handles nested JSON objects by flattening them with dot notation (e.g., address.city). Arrays within values are serialized as text.

All processing happens locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server. No registration, no limits, no tracking.

contentBlocks.title: How does JSON to CSV conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) stores data as structured objects with key-value pairs. CSV (Comma-Separated Values) organizes data in a flat table format with rows and columns. The converter flattens JSON objects into tabular rows, using property names as column headers.

For an array of JSON objects, each object becomes one row in the CSV output. The converter extracts all unique keys from the JSON data and uses them as column headers in the first row. Values are then placed in the corresponding columns for each record.

The resulting CSV uses standard formatting with comma delimiters and proper quoting for values that contain commas, quotes, or newlines. The conversion runs entirely in your browser — no data is sent to any server.

contentBlocks.title: Tips for converting JSON to CSV
contentBlocks.description: A few tips to help you avoid common issues during conversion:

items.title: Nested objects
items.description: CSV is a flat format and cannot represent nested structures. Nested JSON objects are flattened using dot notation (e.g., address.city) or serialized as strings.

items.title: Arrays in values
items.description: If a JSON property contains an array, it will be serialized as a string in the CSV cell. For complex nested data, consider XML or YAML instead of CSV.

items.title: Consistent structure
items.description: For best results, use JSON arrays where all objects have the same keys. Objects with different keys will still work, but missing values will appear as empty cells.

items.title: Large datasets
items.description: The converter handles large JSON files efficiently in the browser. For files over 10 MB, processing may take a few seconds depending on your device.

contentBlocks.title: Handling nested JSON structures
contentBlocks.html:
CSV is a flat tabular format — it cannot natively represent nested objects or arrays. The converter handles nesting in several ways:

  • Nested objects – flattened using dot notation. {"address": {"city": "Warsaw"}} becomes a column called address.city.
  • Arrays of primitives – serialized as comma-separated strings within a single cell.
  • Arrays of objects – serialized as JSON strings within the cell, preserving the structure for manual parsing.
  • Null values – converted to empty cells in CSV.

For best results, use JSON with a flat structure (array of objects with primitive values). Deeply nested JSON may produce many columns with dot-notation headers.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about JSON to CSV conversion

items.question: Is converting JSON to CSV free?
items.answer: Yes, this converter is completely free with no limits. No registration, no watermarks.

items.question: Does the converter upload my files?
items.answer: No. All processing happens locally in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
items.answer: Yes, the converter supports batch conversion. Add multiple files and convert them all simultaneously.

items.question: Does the converter work on mobile?
items.answer: Yes, the converter works on any device with a modern web browser, including smartphones and tablets.

items.question: Is converting JSON to CSV safe?
items.answer: Yes. The entire conversion runs locally in your browser. Your data is never sent to any server. When you close the page, all data is automatically cleared from memory.

items.question: Can I open the CSV output in Excel?
items.answer: Yes. CSV files can be opened directly in Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc, and any other spreadsheet application. The data will appear in rows and columns.

items.question: What happens to nested JSON objects?
items.answer: Nested objects are flattened for the CSV output. Properties are represented using dot notation (e.g., address.city). Arrays within values are serialized as strings.

items.question: Can I convert Stripe or Braintree payment export JSON to CSV?
items.answer: Yes. Stripe and Braintree API responses return JSON arrays of payment or transaction objects. Paste the array directly and the converter will build a CSV with columns for amount, currency, status, customer ID, and any other fields present in the response — ready for your finance team in Excel.
